idea.title: Bandlings
idea.hook: Your monster collection IS the music: every creature on stage plays a looping part on one shared tempo grid, and merging upgrades both the monster and its instrument - the song literally grows as you progress.
idea.prototype: Portrait 4x5 merge stage. Tap the stage to make noise (currency) -> buy eggs -> hatch tier-1 monsters -> drag-merge same species+tier to tier up. Tier decides the instrument lane on ONE AudioContext 8-bar loop: t1 percussion blip, t2 bass, t3 chords/arp, t4 lead, t5 choir. Per-monster patterns derive from a per-player seed, so every player hears their own song. 4 species (timbre + visual families) unlock at milestones; collection book; daily free egg (monotonic clock-cheat guard); rewarded ad = rare egg or 2x set earnings via gf-lib's capped ad path. Remote-config PILOT: egg prices, payouts, daily reward, event flags, motd are live-tunable via GF.remoteConfig.
idea.non_timer_pressure: stage space scarcity (20 cells) + egg price escalation per purchase
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when freeCells()===0 AND no same-species same-tier pair exists (deadlocked stage); set earnings bank into persistent best/collection
idea.why_novel: audible progression as the retention hook - merge games show you progress, this one PLAYS it; first game in the portfolio where audio is the core mechanic, and the designated remote-config pilot

sources.title: Mob Choir - the monster collection IS the music
sources.snippet: Every monster on stage plays a looping musical part on a shared tempo grid; merging upgrades both the creature and its part. The song literally grows as you progress - audible progression is the retention hook.

synthesizer_brief: Manual build authorized by Tim 2026-06-11. Vanilla JS per the A/B alternation rule (previous new game 172_shipwreck_scrub was Phaser); WebAudio core suits vanilla anyway. Rhythm-bonus mechanic CUT from v1 per Codex review (mobile audio/input latency unproven) - parked in DESIGN.md as v2. Offline accrual CUT from v1; daily free egg is the one retention lever.
